title: Prompt: Theme
description: Generates a thematic throughline to give a story its weight.
entries: the cost of being remembered
what we owe the people who made us
how far a person will go to be believed
the difference between forgiveness and forgetting
the lies we tell to keep someone safe
whether a place can love you back
the weight of a promise made to the dead
what survives when everything familiar is gone
the loneliness of being the only one who knows
how grief rearranges a house
the courage it takes to stop running
what we inherit that we never agreed to carry
the quiet violence of being underestimated
how a single choice keeps happening for the rest of a life
the price of belonging somewhere
whether it is braver to leave or to stay
what a person becomes when no one is watching
the stories families tell to survive themselves
how love and control learn to wear each other's clothes
the freedom and the terror of a clean slate
what we destroy in the name of protecting it
the slow work of becoming a stranger to your old self
how the truth arrives too late to be useful
the difference between being needed and being loved
what we are willing to unsee to keep the peace
how power changes the people who claimed they only wanted it once
the debt between who saved you and who you became
whether a name can be outrun
the mercy and the cruelty of a second chance
how home is a thing you can only recognize from far away
